records.ety: [L. aspersio, fr. aspergere: cf. F. aspersion.]

defs: A sprinkling, as with water or dust, in a literal sense.
quotes: Behold an immersion, not and aspersion.

defs: The spreading of calumniations reports or charges which tarnish reputation, like the bespattering of a body with foul water; calumny.
quotes: Every candid critic would be ashamed to cast wholesale aspersions on the entire body of professional teachers.
Who would by base aspersions blot thy virtue.
